message:
test(e2e): fail loudly when hero has no in-flow content (prevent silent vacuous-pass)

The hero overlap specs compute `contentBottom = max(child.getBoundingClientRect().bottom)`
over the hero section's direct children, skipping the indicator itself
and any position: absolute / fixed children. The initial implementation
seeded `contentBottom = -Infinity` and unconditionally returned
`Math.round(contentBottom)`.

Edge case this exposes: if a future refactor wraps the hero's text
content in a position: absolute layer (or the indicator's `md:absolute`
is accidentally broken back to just `absolute`), the for-loop never
updates `contentBottom`, so it stays at `-Infinity`. `Math.round(-Infinity) === -Infinity`,
and Playwright's transport layer serializes -Infinity as JSON `null`.
Back in the Node test runner, `info.contentBottom === null`, so the
assertion reduces to:

    expect(indicator.top).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(null - 1)
    // → expect(indicator.top).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(-1)

which vacuously passes for any non-scrolled indicator — the test
silently greenlights a broken layout.

Fix: track a separate `found` boolean while iterating, and return the
sentinel `{ error: "no in-flow hero content" }` if the loop never
matched a content child. The outer test already throws on any `error`
branch, so the test now fails loudly instead of silently passing.

All 8 articles currently have at least one non-absolute content child
(the EC editorial container), so this is strictly a hardening change —
no current test result flips.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
